notes:
Pokémon Crystal (Chinese). Player 克里斯. Team: Cyndaquil (火球鼠) L6 21/21 HP. Tackle(撞击)/Leer/SmokeScreen. 0 Potions. Objective: Route 29 WEST → Cherrygrove City (吉花市), heal at PC, then north Route 30 → Mr. Pokémon's house (Elm errand).

STATUS: Route 29 — CROSSED the ledge system! Found sandy gap tile in horizontal brown ledge (sand path passes through ledge) and walked UP through it. Now NORTH of ledges. Around me: trees line the north, tree clusters east, vertical white side-ledge far west. Next: find way north/west to main corridor → west to Cherrygrove.

KEY LEARNINGS:
- Ledge gaps = SANDY/light textured tiles interrupting brown round ledge tiles. White ROUND tiles = side-ledge walls (NOT walkable/crossable from west).
- Sprite offset: ledges level with my sprite are actually one row north of my tile.
- First directional press may only turn character (facing), press twice to move.
- NEVER press down near ledges (one-way jumps south into dead-end pockets).
- Battle: A on 战斗, A on 撞击, mash A. Bag: START→背包→item→使用.
summary:
Progress summary: Playing Pokémon Crystal (Chinese) as 克里斯, team: Cyndaquil (火球鼠) now L7, full/near-full HP, 0 Potions. Overall objective: cross Route 29 WEST to Cherrygrove City (吉花市), heal at the Pokémon Center, then go north up Route 30 to Mr. Pokémon's house (Elm's errand).

Where I am: Route 29, on the HIGH/north side of the ledge system at last. Just read a sign confirming "这里是29号" (This is Route 29). The sign sits at the top-left corner of a sandy path region that extends EAST (likely toward New Bark Town). Trees line the north; a wandering NPC is nearby to the south.

What worked (breakthrough): after being stuck south of the ledges, I followed the lower grass lane east, fought and beat a wild Rattata L2 (Cyndaquil leveled to L7), then walked east AROUND the south end of the vertical ledge segment at the ledge corner, then north up the east side — this got me onto the upper terrace with the Route 29 sign. Key learnings that held: first directional press often only turns you (press twice); brown round tiles = south-jump ledges (never press down near them); white round tiles = impassable side-ledge walls; vertical brown strips are cliff walls, not paths — go around their ends.

What didn't work: pressing up directly onto the vertical brown strip (blocked); earlier attempts to cross the horizontal ledge line from the south all failed except going around the east corner.

What I just did: pressed left×4 heading WEST from the sign along the tree line (west should lead toward Cherrygrove; the sandy path east likely returns to New Bark).

Immediate next objective: continue WEST along Route 29's upper corridor, hugging the north tree line, watching for the route to open toward Cherrygrove City. If blocked west, probe for openings north through the trees. In battles: A on 战斗, A on 撞击, mash A.
